title: C Output
content:
### Output in C

The `printf()` function from `<stdio.h>` is used to print output to the console.

### Format Specifiers

| Specifier | Data Type |
|-----------|-----------|
| `%d` or `%i` | `int` |
| `%f` | `float` / `double` |
| `%c` | `char` |
| `%s` | string (char array) |
| `%u` | unsigned int |
| `%ld` | long int |
| `%lf` | double |
| `%x` | hexadecimal |
| `%p` | pointer address |

### Escape Sequences

| Sequence | Meaning |
|----------|---------|
| `\n` | Newline |
| `\t` | Horizontal tab |
| `\\` | Backslash |
| `\"` | Double quote |
| `\'` | Single quote |
| `\r` | Carriage return |

---

> **Key Point:** Use the correct format specifier matching the variable type to avoid undefined behavior.

quiz.question: Which format specifier is used for integers in printf?
